content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA of size N×NN \times NN×N.

You need to rotate the matrix AAA by 180180180 degrees, either in clockwise or anti-clockwise direction.

Check the samples below for clarity.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a N+1N + 1N+1 lines of input.

First line of each test case contains the size of the matrix NNN.
This is followed by NNN lines - Each line of input contains NNN space-separated integers which form the N×NN \times NN×N matrix.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines, where each line has NNN space-separated integers, denoting the matrix after rotating it 180180180 degrees.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N – the size of the square matrix. The next N lines each contain N space‑separated integers representing the matrix rows.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N lines. Each line contains N space‑separated integers representing the matrix after a 180° rotation (equivalently, rows are printed in reverse order and each row is printed in reverse order).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each test case matrix is printed after a 180° rotation: rows are reversed and each row is reversed.
Reversing rows and columns yields the rotated matrix.

solution.approach: For a 180° rotation the element that was at position (i, j) moves to position (N‑1‑i, N‑1‑j). The simplest way is to read the whole matrix, then output it starting from the last row to the first and, inside each row, from the last column to the first. This requires O(N^2) time and O(N^2) auxiliary space for storing the matrix (the space can be avoided by printing while reading, but storing keeps the code simple).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) – each element is read once and printed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) – to store one matrix at a time.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        mat = [[int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)] for __ in range(N)]
        for i in range(N-1, -1, -1):
            row = mat[i][::-1]
            out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, row)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
